title: Raid on Yirt-4138-Grek-12

{{Quote|We will do all we can to protect our assets and intellectual properties.|Corporate Alliance Magistrate Passel Argente, following the raid|<ref name="AllianceAttacks" />}}
In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], during the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], the [[Corporate Alliance/Legends|Corporate Alliance]] conducted a '''raid''' against a [[Cyborg slicers of Yirt-4138-Grek-12|group]] of [[Slicer/Legends|slicers]] on an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] named [[Yirt-4138-Grek-12]]. Upon learning of a secret [[Yirt-4138-Grek-12 slicer compound|slicer compound]] on the asteroid used to supply [[Spy/Legends|espionage agents]] who were stealing data from the Corporate Alliance, the corporation deployed its [[NR-N99 Persuader-class droid enforcer/Legends|NR-N99 ''Persuader''-class droid enforcers]] on the asteroid, and the [[Droid tank/Legends|droid tanks]] attacked the base. Following the attack, twenty-five slicers lay [[Death/Legends|dead]], and the base was annihilated. While some [[Senator/Legends|senators]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] believed the attack to be excessive, it nonetheless fell within legal boundaries.

==Prelude==
{{Quote|The compound contained unauthorized cybernetic surgery suites that were churning out vessels and cyborgs that have been slicing into Corporate Alliance data-stores.|Passel Argente|<ref name="AllianceAttacks" />}}
During the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], a secret industrial [[Spy/Legends|espionage]] training [[Yirt-4138-Grek-12 slicer compound|center]] was located on a remote [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] named [[Yirt-4138-Grek-12]] in the [[Corporate Alliance/Legends|Corporate Alliance]]–controlled [[Fakir sector/Legends|Fakir sector]]. The base was used to supply and train industrial espionage agents by building them [[Starship/Legends|starships]] and performing [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] surgeries on them, thus turning them into [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborgs]]. The base's operations were unauthorized by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and, as such, were kept hidden by the parties who funded the facility.<ref name="AllianceAttacks" />

One of the base's agents was [[Denlace Sachoore]], a twenty-four-[[Standard year/Legends|year]]-old [[Gender/Legends|female]] who underwent surgery and came out with a [[Bone/Legends|cranium]]-implanted [[vessel drive]]. However, while working as a Corporate Alliance clerk, she was caught embezzling corporate data in the vessel drive and was questioned by Corporate Alliance [[expeditor]]s. Approximately a [[Standard month/Legends|month]] later, the source of her training and surgery was traced to Yirt-4138-Grek-12, and the facility was discovered. Viewing the base as a potential threat to corporate revenues, the [[Corporate Alliance Directorate]], led by [[Magistrate/Legends|Magistrate]] [[Passel Argente/Legends|Passel Argente]], decided in a 7–2 vote that instant eliminating actions were required.<ref name="AllianceAttacks" />

==The raid==
{{Quote|This potential revenue-threat has been neutralized.|Passel Argente|<ref name="AllianceAttacks" />}}
[[File:TankDroidHolonet.png|thumb|right|250px|The Corporate Alliance used NR-N99 ''Persuader''-class droid enforcers to destroy the slicing facility.]]
The Corporate Alliance sent [[Fantail-class destroyer/Legends|transports]] containing [[NR-N99 Persuader-class droid enforcer/Legends|NR-N99 ''Persuader''-class droid enforcers]] to Yirt-4138-Grek-12, deploying them ten [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] from the base. The [[Droid tank/Legends|droid tanks]] then proceeded to travel across the asteroid to the facility, where they breached the pressurized structure by using their [[Laser/Legends|laser]] and [[Missile/Legends|missile]] weaponry, and, as a result, [[Cyborg slicers of Yirt-4138-Grek-12|twenty-five cybernetically-enhanced data thieves]] [[Death/Legends|perished]] inside the base, which was completely destroyed.<ref name="AllianceAttacks" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|It's all in the clear, as far as the letter of the law goes. But it couldn't be more retrograde to the spirit of the law. Since the asteroid was in Alliance space, and clearly in their jurisdiction, though, there's nothing wrong with what they did, legally speaking.|Senator Gopple|<ref name="AllianceAttacks" />}}
After the raid, Argente dispatched a [[Holomessage/Legends|holomessage]] to Corporate Alliance shareholders, in which he explained the details of the raiding mission and assured that the corporation would do everything it could to protect its business interests. The raid was later reported by [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]]. While the raid was perfectly legal, as it took place in Corporate Alliance [[Realspace/Legends|space]], a number of [[Senator/Legends|senators]] of the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]], including Senator [[Gopple]] of [[Erigorm]], objected to the attack, believing that the use of [[Armor/Legends|armored]] [[Tank/Legends|tank]] [[Droid/Legends|droids]] was too extreme.<ref name="AllianceAttacks" />

==Behind the scenes==
The raid was first mentioned in the [[in-universe]] [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 48|''HoloNet News Vol. 531 #48'']] article ''Corporate Alliance Attacks Slicer Compound'', part of the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News]] volume<ref name="AllianceAttacks" /> released on [[March 21]], [[2002]].<ref name="JFC">{{JCFCite|url=posts/6128630|board=Literature|thread=The Official HoloNetNews Discussion Thread|user=PrinceXizor|date=2002-03-21|time=3:24 PM|archiveurl=https://archive.is/lQRg7|quote=Issue 48 is up !}}</ref>
